title: LilyGO device UI for LICHEN firmware
description: Why this exists: LilyGO-class Meshtastic-compatible boards are likely first-run contributor/user hardware, and the Zephyr firmware needs an on-device UI path for status, pairing, diagnostics, and actual user messaging rather than relying only on serial logs or phone apps. What needs to be done: design and implement the LilyGO UI surface for supported display/button/GPS variants, including boot/status screens, node identity display, radio/link status, neighbor/route summary, text messaging views, settings affordances, error states, and low-power behavior. The UI must act as a basic texting app: browse channels/groups, read message history, compose/send channel/group messages, browse contacts/peers, read 1:1 conversations, compose/send 1:1 messages, show delivery/pending/error state where available, and surface unread/message notification state. Integrate with existing firmware abstractions without hard-coding a single board layout; consume firmware-wide location and date/time providers instead of owning GNSS/time source policy. Acceptance criteria: supported LilyGO boards are enumerated; display/input drivers and board overlays are documented; UI state model and rendering/input loop cover status plus channel/group and 1:1 messaging; message compose constraints for small screens/buttons are documented; simulator or hardware test path is documented; build succeeds for at least one LilyGO target; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes:
Location/GNSS is firmware-wide, not LilyGO-specific. LilyGO UI must consume the unified location provider tracked by project-LICHEN-zte2 and render fix/source/age/quality from that provider instead of assuming onboard GNSS.
Design direction from 2026-06-30: do not build the LilyGO UI stack from scratch. Use Zephyr's display subsystem and input subsystem as the hardware boundary, and use Zephyr's bundled LVGL integration where the selected LilyGO display/MCU has enough RAM/refresh capability for it. Keep the LICHEN-specific work in the app state model, screen flow, styles, widgets, and HAL bindings. For constrained or slow-refresh boards such as e-ink targets, allow a thinner display-API renderer if LVGL is too heavy or mismatched, but still keep drivers/input under Zephyr subsystems.
